tagline: Augentik is the first full-stack, AI-native insurance carrier built for technology startups. We own underwriting, pricing, policy administration, claims, and reinsurance, with no broker in the middle. Founders get a full quote and bound certificate in under 10 minutes.
company_description: Augentik is the first full-stack, AI-native insurance carrier built for technology startups. We own underwriting, pricing, policy administration, claims, and reinsurance, with no broker in the middle. Founders get a full quote and bound certificate in under 10 minutes. Tagline: business insurance at the speed of compute.
problem: Startups move at a pace the insurance industry was never designed to serve. Traditional insurance takes 3–6 weeks, involves 8–12 human touchpoints, and runs on legacy systems built for corporate risk managers, not founders closing seed rounds or signing enterprise contracts.
solution: A full-stack insurance carrier with AI-native underwriting, modular coverage (GL, D&O, Tech E&O, Cyber, EPLI, Media Liability, AI Liability, Hired & Non-Owned Auto, Fiduciary Liability, Reps & Warranties), and a digital-first experience built for technical founders.
target_market: Venture-backed technology startups, pre-seed through Series B+, in 49 U.S. states. Trigger events: funding rounds, enterprise contracts, first hires, lease signings.
traction: 40,000+ active customers. $40M+ ARR as of December 2025. Full carrier license July 2025. Captive reinsurer license late 2025. $108M raised at $630M valuation January 2026. Under 1% annual churn.
business_model: Annual premiums collected directly as a licensed carrier. Revenue from underwriting profit and investment income on float. Average customer pays roughly $1,000/year.
thesis: The insurance industry is a $1T+ market running on 1970s infrastructure. AI changes unit economics: manual underwriting that took weeks can run in seconds when the stack is native. Startups are the fastest-growing and most underserved segment. We invested in licenses so we could rebuild the stack instead of bolting onto legacy.
differentiators: Full-stack carrier (not broker or MGA). Sub-10 minute quote-to-bound. AI-native underwriting, modular coverage, AI Liability category leadership, founder-first UX, ownership of risk and float. Instrumented quote-to-bind and claims feedback loop to defend unit economics.
founder_background: CEO & CTO. Columbia (Rabi Scholar). Previously founded Basket (gaming publisher at 200M+ MAU scale). Forbes 30 Under 30 (2024). Co-founder Emily Yuan (COO): Stanford CS, operations and GTM. ~70 person team; SF HQ plus Salt Lake City, Dallas, Chicago, Atlanta.
icp: Venture-backed tech startups, pre-seed through Series B+, 49 U.S. states
Founders who need speed: post-funding D&O, pre-enterprise cyber, first-hire EPLI
Technical buyers who want clarity, not a 40-page PDF from a broker
priorities: Productionize the AI underwriting feedback loop: quote → bind → claim → root cause → model and rules signals (closed-loop telemetry)
Defend unit economics: bind rate on quotes, post-bind declinations, segment loss ratios, data mismatch detection
GTM scale: partnerships with YC, a16z, Sequoia, and similar for portfolio distribution
Enterprise readiness: Client Success capacity for Series B+ accounts alongside digital-first core
risks: Regulatory complexity across 49 states; a major state dispute could slow growth
Reinsurance and capital markets; hard markets compress margin
Underwriting accuracy: mispriced segments or bind-then-decline from misclassified risk
Talent: rare mix of insurance regulation depth and modern engineering
Brand and distribution outside core founder networks without deliberate GTM
